summary: Iran announced that the US‑Iran cease‑fire applies to all fronts, including Lebanon, and later launched missiles toward Kuwait with no reported damage. The United States intercepted two ballistic missiles aimed at its base in Kuwait and conducted self‑defence strikes on Iranian radar sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island. Israel reported multiple UAV and rocket incursions from Lebanon, intercepted them, and carried out strikes in Gaza and Tyre, killing a senior Hamas commander and causing civilian casualties at Jabal Amel Hospital.
key_points: Iran’s foreign minister declared the US‑Iran cease‑fire a blanket cease‑fire on all fronts, including Lebanon, and warned that any breach on one front violates the whole cease‑fire (Source: @pkpoi/142349).
Iran launched missiles toward Kuwait, triggering alarms but causing no reported damage or casualties (Source: @pkpoi/142352).
The US intercepted two ballistic missiles launched toward its base in Kuwait, with no injuries reported (Source: @asafroz/49734).

Israel’s IDF reported UAV intrusions from Lebanon over Rosh Hanikra and the southern coast, intercepting the aircraft and three rockets without casualties (Source: @New_security8200/200790; @New_security8200/200767).
Hezbollah fired rockets at Safed, which were intercepted, prompting sirens in multiple towns (Source: @manniefabian/58356).
An Israeli strike in Gaza killed Hamas platoon commander Jamal Abu Aoun, a doctor at Yafa Hospital (Source: @manniefabian/58358).
Israeli airstrikes hit Jabal Amel Hospital in Tyre, causing civilian casualties (Source: @presstv/192236).
IRGC warned that crossing red lines in Lebanon and Gaza would be treated as direct war, mentioning new fronts and the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @presstv/192241).
US Central Command conducted self‑defence strikes on Iranian radar and command sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3225).

Israel’s 146 Division completed its third combat tour in south Lebanon, claiming over 550 militants killed and more than 2,700 terror infrastructure targets destroyed (Source: @New_security8200/200731).

sections.executive_summary: Iran’s foreign minister declared the US‑Iran cease‑fire a blanket cease‑fire covering Lebanon, while Iran launched missiles toward Kuwait with no damage reported. The United States intercepted two ballistic missiles aimed at its base in Kuwait and struck Iranian radar sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island. Israel reported multiple UAV and rocket incursions from Lebanon, intercepted them, and conducted strikes in Gaza and Tyre, killing a senior Hamas commander and causing civilian casualties at Jabal Amel Hospital.

points: Iran’s foreign minister Abas Araqchi stated that the US‑Iran cease‑fire is a cease‑fire on all fronts, including Lebanon, and that any violation on one front breaches the entire cease‑fire (Source: @pkpoi/142349).
Missiles were fired from Iran toward Kuwait, triggering nationwide alarms; no damage or casualties were reported (Source: @pkpoi/142352).
The United States intercepted two ballistic missiles launched toward its base in Kuwait, with no injuries (Source: @asafroz/49734).
US Central Command reported self‑defence strikes on Iranian radar and command‑and‑control sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island in response to a downed US MQ‑1 drone (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3225).

IRGC intelligence warned that crossing red lines in Lebanon and Gaza would be treated as direct war, mentioning decisive actions, opening new fronts, and maintaining the Strait of Hormuz equation (Source: @presstv/192241).
The IDF reported UAV intrusions from Lebanon over the Rosh Hanikra and HaBustan coastal areas, intercepting the unmanned aircraft (Source: @New_security8200/200790).
Three rockets launched from Lebanon toward Israeli territory were intercepted, with no injuries reported (Source: @New_security8200/200767).
Hezbollah rockets aimed at Safed were intercepted, triggering sirens in multiple towns (Source: @manniefabian/58356).
An Israeli strike in Gaza killed Hamas platoon commander Jamal Abu Aoun, who also served as a doctor at Yafa Hospital (Source: @manniefabian/58358).
Israeli airstrikes on Jabal Amel Hospital in Tyre caused civilian casualties (Source: @presstv/192236).

Israel’s 146 Division completed its third combat tour in south Lebanon, reporting over 550 militants killed and more than 2,700 terror infrastructure targets destroyed (Source: @New_security8200/200731).
Israeli forces killed Hamas commander Jamal Abu Aoun in a strike in the Gaza Strip; he was identified as a platoon commander and doctor at Yafa Hospital (Source: @manniefabian/58358).
Airstrikes on Jabal Amel Hospital in Tyre, while not in Gaza, were reported to have caused civilian casualties, indicating broader regional targeting (Source: @presstv/192236).
Hezbollah launched rockets toward Safed, which were intercepted by Israeli defenses, prompting sirens across several towns (Source: @manniefabian/58356).

Multiple UAV and rocket incursions from Lebanon were intercepted by Israeli forces over Rosh Hanikra and other northern locations, with no reported injuries (Source: @New_security8200/200790; @New_security8200/200767).
Iran’s foreign minister linked the US‑Iran cease‑fire to Lebanon, stating any breach there would violate the overall cease‑fire (Source: @pkpoi/142349).
IRGC warned that repeated Israeli violations in Lebanon could lead to opening new fronts and direct war (Source: @presstv/192241).
A video from Omidiyeh in Iran’s Khuzestan province showed a missile launch at 06:30 local time on 1 June 2026 (Source: @shin_persian/6348).
Reports from Iran described simultaneous explosions in Tehran and Kermanshah attributed to gas leaks, with injuries reported (Source: @VahidOnline/75824).
Iran launched missiles toward Kuwait, triggering alarms but causing no reported damage (Source: @pkpoi/142352).
The United States intercepted two ballistic missiles aimed at its base in Kuwait, with no casualties (Source: @asafroz/49734).
US Central Command conducted self‑defence strikes on Iranian radar sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3225).

key_developments: Iran’s foreign minister declared the US‑Iran cease‑fire a blanket cease‑fire covering all fronts, including Lebanon, warning that any breach nullifies the entire cease‑fire (Source: @pkpoi/142349).
Iran launched missiles toward Kuwait, activating nationwide alarms but resulting in no reported damage or casualties (Source: @pkpoi/142352).
The United States intercepted two ballistic missiles aimed at its base in Kuwait, with no injuries reported (Source: @asafroz/49734).

Israel’s IDF intercepted multiple UAVs and three rockets originating from Lebanon over Rosh Hanikra and other northern areas, reporting no injuries (Source: @New_security8200/200790; @New_security8200/200767).
Hezbollah rockets aimed at Safed were intercepted, triggering sirens in numerous towns (Source: @manniefabian/58356).
An Israeli strike in Gaza killed Hamas platoon commander and doctor Jamal Abu Aoun (Source: @manniefabian/58358).
Israeli airstrikes on Jabal Amel Hospital in Tyre caused civilian casualties (Source: @presstv/192236).
sections.threat_assessment: The region faces heightened multi‑front tensions as Iran links its cease‑fire with Lebanon, while simultaneously launching missiles toward Kuwait and facing US interceptions. Israel confronts repeated aerial and rocket incursions from Hezbollah in Lebanon, responding with interceptions and strikes, and escalates operations in Gaza, targeting senior Hamas figures. US actions against Iranian radar sites indicate a willingness to conduct pre‑emptive strikes, raising the risk of broader confrontation, especially around the Strait of Hormuz.
sections.regional_response: Iran’s diplomatic stance ties any violation in Lebanon to the broader US‑Iran cease‑fire, signaling potential retaliation across the region. Israel’s intensified air defence and offensive operations in Lebanon and Gaza aim to neutralise immediate threats but risk further civilian casualties and international criticism. The United States is actively defending its assets in the Gulf, intercepting missiles and striking Iranian command sites, suggesting a coordinated effort with regional allies to contain Iranian aggression.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile trajectories from Iran toward Gulf states should be intensified, given recent launches toward Kuwait. UAV and rocket activity from Lebanon requires continued radar coverage and rapid interception capabilities to prevent escalation. The reported casualties from Israeli strikes in Tyre and the killing of a senior Hamas commander may provoke retaliatory attacks from Hezbollah and Hamas, necessitating heightened alert levels in border and urban areas.
